name: wilson-inbox
description: Cross-project handoff inbox — when a gap or request affects another SSOT repo, file it as a structured entry in that repo's inbox/<kind>/<slug>.md (kind: notes / patches / poc / rfc_drafts) instead of silently working around it downstream. /wilson-inbox:inbox scaffolds and manages entries: add / list / show / path / verify / apply / archive / rm / pr. The pr verb takes a scaffolded entry through the full upstream loop in one idempotent step: checkout (or create) `inbox/<kind>/<slug>` branch in the target repo, stage + commit the entry, push -u, then `gh pr create` with the file's first H1 as the PR title and the file body as the PR body. Repo resolution: --to <name> targets ~/core/<name>, else the nearest .git walking up from the cwd. Light-mode is just the folder + entries; heavy-mode adds inbox/PATCHES.yaml with a status lifecycle that apply/archive transition. A SessionStart hook surfaces the current repo's pending inbox entries so a cross-project handoff is never forgotten — inert unless the repo has an inbox/. Standalone port of wilson's inbox plugin. Default ON. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_INBOX=1.
